info.name: Microsoft Azure ManagementLockClient
info.description: Azure resources can be locked to prevent other users in your organization from deleting or modifying resources.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Providers Microsoft Authorization Operations

request.description: Lists all of the available Microsoft.Authorization REST API operations.

response.name: OK. The request has succeeded.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Creates Or Updates A Management Lock At The Resource Group Level

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group to lock.
(Required) The lock name. The lock name can be a maximum of 260 characters. It cannot contain <, > %, &, :, \, ?, /, or any control characters.

request.description: When you apply a lock at a parent scope, all child resources inherit the same lock. To create management locks, you must have access to Microsoft.Authorization/* or Microsoft.Authorization/locks/* actions. Of the built-in roles, only Owner and User Access Administrator are granted those actions.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the lock.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Created - Returns information about the lock.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Deletes A Management Lock At The Resource Group Level

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group containing the lock.
(Required) The name of lock to delete.

request.description: To delete management locks, you must have access to Microsoft.Authorization/* or Microsoft.Authorization/locks/* actions. Of the built-in roles, only Owner and User Access Administrator are granted those actions.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: NoContent

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Authorization Locks Lockname

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the locked resource group.
(Required) The name of the lock to get.

request.description: Gets a management lock at the resource group level.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the lock.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: The filter to apply on the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group containing the locks to get.

request.description: Gets all the management locks for a resource group.

response.name: OK - Returns an array of resource locks.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: The filter to apply on the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: The filter to apply on the operation.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Creates Or Updates A Management Lock At The Resource Level Or Any Level Below The Resource

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group containing the resource to lock.
(Required) The resource provider namespace of the resource to lock.
(Required) The parent resource identity.
(Required) The resource type of the resource to lock.
(Required) The name of the resource to lock.
(Required) The name of lock. The lock name can be a maximum of 260 characters. It cannot contain <, > %, &, :, \, ?, /, or any control characters.

request.description: When you apply a lock at a parent scope, all child resources inherit the same lock. To create management locks, you must have access to Microsoft.Authorization/* or Microsoft.Authorization/locks/* actions. Of the built-in roles, only Owner and User Access Administrator are granted those actions.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the lock.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Created - Returns information about the lock.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Deletes The Management Lock Of A Resource Or Any Level Below The Resource

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group containing the resource with the lock to delete.
(Required) The resource provider namespace of the resource with the lock to delete.
(Required) The parent resource identity.
(Required) The resource type of the resource with the lock to delete.
(Required) The name of the resource with the lock to delete.
(Required) The name of the lock to delete.

request.description: To delete management locks, you must have access to Microsoft.Authorization/* or Microsoft.Authorization/locks/* actions. Of the built-in roles, only Owner and User Access Administrator are granted those actions.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: NoContent

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Resourceprovidernamespace Parentresourcepath Resourcetype Resourcename Providers Microsoft Authorization Locks Lockname

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group.
(Required) The namespace of the resource provider.
(Required) An extra path parameter needed in some services, like SQL Databases.
(Required) The type of the resource.
(Required) The name of the resource.
(Required) The name of lock.

request.description: Get the management lock of a resource or any level below resource.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Resourceprovidernamespace Parentresourcepath Resourcetype Resourcename Providers Microsoft Authorization Locks

query.description: The filter to apply on the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the resource group containing the locked resource. The name is case insensitive.
(Required) The namespace of the resource provider.
(Required) The parent resource identity.
(Required) The resource type of the locked resource.
(Required) The name of the locked resource.

request.description: Gets all the management locks for a resource or any level below resource.

response.name: OK - Returns an array of resource locks.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: The filter to apply on the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

query.description: The filter to apply on the operation.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Put Scope Providers Microsoft Authorization Locks Lockname

variable.description: (Required) The scope for the lock. When providing a scope for the assignment, use '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}' for subscriptions, '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourcegroups/{resourceGroupName}' for resource groups, and '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourcegroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{parentResourcePathIfPresent}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}' for resources.
(Required) The name of lock.

request.description: Create or update a management lock by scope.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Created

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
